model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds a commanding ~18-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Georgia (KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38 vs. KenPom #31, AdjEM +20.47), anchored by the nation's 2nd-best defense (AdjDE 88.4) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize a Bulldogs offense ranked 15th nationally — the most critical matchup asymmetry in this game. Georgia's own defense ranked 93rd nationally (AdjDE 104.2) is a severe liability against Michigan's top-8 offense, and their 22-10 record, while SEC-tested, reflects a team operating well below Michigan's elite two-way profile. Yaxel Lendeborg's low ankle sprain was confirmed as non-disqualifying for the first round, and with additional recovery days between games Michigan enters near full strength with Dusty May's elite defensive system presenting a matchup Georgia has no credible answer for. The combination of Michigan's superior efficiency on both ends of the floor, Big Ten battle-testing, and the historical ~85% win rate for 1-seeds in the Round of 32 make the Wolverines a clear and confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan vs. Georgia — Round of 32 Midwest Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan is a dominant #1 seed with an ~18-point efficiency gap over Georgia. The Wolverines' elite defense (2nd nationally) is the decisive matchup factor against a Bulldogs team with a leaky defense ranked 93rd nationally. Lendeborg's ankle injury is manageable with added recovery time, and Georgia's SEC credentials are real but insufficient to overcome this quality gap in single-elimination play.
evidence: Michigan KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38), Torvik #2 — nation's 2nd-best defense (AdjDE 88.4), top-8 offense (AdjOE 126.7), record 31-2
Georgia KenPom #31 (AdjEM +20.47), Torvik #37 — offense ranked 15th nationally (AdjOE 124.7) but defense ranked 93rd (AdjDE 104.2), record 22-10
Efficiency gap of ~18 points is historically very difficult to overcome in single-elimination play
Yaxel Lendeborg (low ankle sprain) confirmed available for NCAA opener; additional recovery time between rounds improves his effectiveness
Georgia's defensive ranking (93rd nationally) is a severe liability against Michigan's top-8 offense
Historical 1-seed win rate in Round of 32 is approximately 85%; Michigan's profile is among the strongest 1-seeds in the bracket

modelTrace.content:
I now have all the data I need to make a well-informed pick. Let me synthesize the findings:

**Key Data Summary:**

- **Michigan**: KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38), Torvik #2, record 31-2 (31-3 per MSN snippet after Big Ten Tournament loss to Purdue). Nation's 2nd-best defense (AdjDE 88.4/90.5), top-8 offense (AdjOE 126.7/127.7). Lendeborg had a low ankle sprain but confirmed he expected to play in the opener — by Round of 32 he's had additional recovery time.

- **Georgia**: KenPom #31 (AdjEM +20.47), Torvik #37, record 22-10. Offense ranked 15th-18th nationally (AdjOE 124.7/124.3), but defense ranked 93rd nationally (AdjDE 104.2/104.7) — a severe liability. Per prior pick, Georgia beat Saint Louis as an 8/9 coin-flip.

- **Efficiency gap**: ~18 points (Michigan +38.38 vs. Georgia +20.47) — a commanding advantage for the #1 seed.

- **Matchup asymmetry**: Michigan's 2nd-best defense in the nation runs directly into Georgia's offense ranked 15th — a tough assignment for the Bulldogs. Georgia's defense ranked 93rd nationally is a catastrophic liability against Michigan's top-8 offense.

- **Injury context**: Lendeborg's ankle sprain was minor (low ankle, expected to play in opener), and with additional days of rest between Round of 64 and Round of 32, his availability and effectiveness should be improved. No significant Georgia injury concerns surfaced.

- **Schedule strength**: Michigan's Big Ten battle-testing is elite; Georgia's SEC résumé is solid but their 22-10 record and defensive deficiencies are a clear step down.

- **Historical context**: 1-seeds advance past 8/9-seed winners at roughly an 85%+ rate historically.

    "confidence": 0.88,
    "rationale": "Michigan holds a commanding ~18-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Georgia (KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38 vs. KenPom #31, AdjEM +20.47), anchored by the nation's 2nd-best defense (AdjDE 88.4) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize a Bulldogs offense ranked 15th nationally — the most critical matchup asymmetry in this game. Georgia's own defense ranked 93rd nationally (AdjDE 104.2) is a severe liability against Michigan's top-8 offense, and their 22-10 record, while SEC-tested, reflects a team operating well below Michigan's elite two-way profile. Yaxel Lendeborg's low ankle sprain was already confirmed as non-disqualifying for the first round, and with additional recovery days between games, Michigan enters near full strength with Dusty May's elite defensive system presenting a matchup Georgia has no credible answer for. The combination of Michigan's superior efficiency on both ends of the floor, Big Ten battle-testing, and the historical ~85% win rate for 1-seeds in the Round of 32 make the Wolverines a clear and confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16."
